label: verify-54952-2
system:
You are the citation verifier in a legal-exploration pipeline. You are given one authority as cited by the pipeline's components, plus snippets showing how it was used. Using web search (prefer primary/official sources: leginfo.legislature.ca.gov, courts.ca.gov, law.justia.com, casetext.com, codes.findlaw.com, fppc.ca.gov), determine:
1. Does the authority exist?
2. Is the citation form correct (reporter cite, year, section number)?
3. Does it plausibly stand for what the snippets use it for?

Statuses: VERIFIED (exists, cite essentially right, usage consistent with what you found); MISCHARACTERIZED (exists but does not support the claimed use, or the cite attaches to the wrong case/section); NOT_FOUND (cannot locate after genuine searching); CONTESTED (real sources point in conflicting directions, or scope is genuinely unsettled); UNCLEAR (could not determine within a reasonable search).

You are NOT determining whether the authority is still good law (no Shepardizing) — say what you actually confirmed, no more. Be strict: a close-but-wrong reporter cite or a holding stretched past what sources show is MISCHARACTERIZED, not VERIFIED.
prompt:

[tetlock] …y-estimated cruxes, recompose, and name the decisive crux (forecasting-tournament style).

|---|---|---|
| **SQ1**: Does a serial meeting under Gov. Code § 54952.2(b)(1) require that a member intend, or at least know, that a communication will reach or be aggregated among a majority of the body, or is the prohibition triggered by the objective fact that a series of communications functi…

[dialectic] …fies even the more demanding "collective concurrence" framework courts have applied to indirect/serial contacts, and is stronger than the facts in the governing appellate authority.

---

## II. Statutory framework

- **Gov. Code § 54952.2(a)** — general definition of "meeting" (congregation of a majority to discuss, deliberate, or act).
- **Gov. Code § 54952.2(b)(1)** — the serial-meeting clause: a majority "shall not... use a series of communications of an…

## Context

The underlying question is a California public-agency hypothetical: q1 — The AI assistant as serial-meeting intermediary (Brown Act).

Verify this authority per your instructions.

canonical_cite.description: the correct full citation, if determinable

note.description: 1-3 sentences: what was confirmed or refuted, and the correct cite

text: {"status":"VERIFIED","canonical_cite":"Cal. Gov't Code § 54952.2 (Ralph M. Brown Act) — subsec. (a) (definition of \"meeting\"), (b)(1) (serial-meeting/series-of-communications prohibition), (b)(2) (staff/employee informational-contact safe harbor), (c) (enumerated exceptions, e.g., conferences, social/ceremonial events)","note":"Confirmed via leginfo.legislature.ca.gov and secondary sources (FindLaw, Justia, Brown Act practice guides): § 54952.2(a) is the \"meeting\" definition, (b)(1) is the serial-communications/serial-meeting prohibition, (b)(2) is the staff-contact carve-out (\"does not communicate... the comments or position of any other member\"), and (c) lists exceptions — exactly matching the pipeline's usage in all three documents. Checked SB 707 (chaptered Oct. 3, 2025, eff. Jan. 1, 2026, current as of the July 2026 date in this session): it kept (a), (b)(1), (b)(2) lettering intact and only added a permanent (b)(3) for social media, so the subsection lettering cited is still current and the [delphi] doc's caution to \"verify exact current subsection lettering\" is satisfied rather than contradicted.","source_urls":["https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displaySection.xhtml?sectionNum=54952.2.&lawCode=GOV","https://codes.findlaw.com/ca/government-code/gov-sect-54952-2/","https://law.justia.com/codes/california/2020/code-gov/title-5/division-2/part-1/chapter-9/section-54952-2/","https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/billTextClient.xhtml?bill_id=202520260SB707","https://www.bwslaw.com/insights/public-law-update-sb-707/"]}

data.canonical_cite: Cal. Gov't Code § 54952.2 (Ralph M. Brown Act) — subsec. (a) (definition of "meeting"), (b)(1) (serial-meeting/series-of-communications prohibition), (b)(2) (staff/employee informational-contact safe harbor), (c) (enumerated exceptions, e.g., conferences, social/ceremonial events)
data.note: Confirmed via leginfo.legislature.ca.gov and secondary sources (FindLaw, Justia, Brown Act practice guides): § 54952.2(a) is the "meeting" definition, (b)(1) is the serial-communications/serial-meeting prohibition, (b)(2) is the staff-contact carve-out ("does not communicate... the comments or position of any other member"), and (c) lists exceptions — exactly matching the pipeline's usage in all three documents. Checked SB 707 (chaptered Oct. 3, 2025, eff. Jan. 1, 2026, current as of the July 2026 date in this session): it kept (a), (b)(1), (b)(2) lettering intact and only added a permanent (b)(3) for social media, so the subsection lettering cited is still current and the [delphi] doc's caution to "verify exact current subsection lettering" is satisfied rather than contradicted.
